deployment_setup_phase.overview: This phase handles the initial deployment option selection and project setup. It must be completed BEFORE proceeding to API discovery and configuration generation.

phase_0_deployment_selection.title: Deployment Option Selection & Setup
phase_0_deployment_selection.description: Before creating REST API connector configuration files, determine the deployment approach and set up the necessary project structure.

step_1_ask_deployment_option.title: Ask User for Deployment Preference
step_1_ask_deployment_option.description: Present the user with two deployment options and gather their preference.
step_1_ask_deployment_option.question: Which deployment option would you like to use for this REST API connector?

option.name: codeless
option.label: Codeless Option
option.description: Download a pre-built Docker image from the repository. Quick setup with no code generation required. Best for: Standard use cases without customization needs.

option.name: developer
option.label: Developer Option
option.description: Generate connector code that you can modify and customize. Build your own Docker image from the generated code. Best for: Custom implementations and advanced use cases.

step_1_ask_deployment_option.presentation_format: Present options clearly with numbered choices: 1. Codeless Option - Download pre-built Docker image 2. Developer Option - Generate and customize connector code

action.name: Create Project Structure
action.description: Create the necessary directory structure for storing DSL configuration files.
action.note: This structure will hold the DSL configuration files (JSON connector descriptors) that will be created in subsequent phases.

action.name: Inform User About Codeless Option
action.message:
The codeless option will use a pre-built Docker image from Docker Hub.
<<<<<<< HEAD <<<<<<< HEAD <<<<<<< HEAD:.bob/rules-connectors-forge-rest/0_deployment_setup.xml Docker Image: ghcr.io/marek-zuwala/connectors-forge:1.0.1 ======= Docker Image: marek02/connectors-forge:latest >>>>>>> 5b82670 (mode can do everything):.bob/rules-rest-api-schema-mapper/0_deployment_setup.xml ======= Docker Image: ghcr.io/marek-zuwala/connectors-forge:1.0.0 >>>>>>> dacf80e (custom mode can able to deploy on openshift) ======= Docker Image: ghcr.io/marek-zuwala/connectors-forge:1.0.1 >>>>>>> 9083115 (update image tag)
This pre-built image contains all necessary components to run your REST API connectors. When you proceed to deployment (Phase 5), you will use this image instead of building your own.
The project structure has been created at: sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest/src/main/resources/
Your DSL configuration files will be stored in this location and will be used with the pre-built Docker image during deployment.

action.name: Proceed to Configuration
action.description: After informing the user, proceed to Phase 1 (API Discovery & Analysis) to gather API information and create configuration files.

sub_step_1_ask_generation_preference.title: Ask Code Generation Preference
sub_step_1_ask_generation_preference.question: Would you like me to generate the connector code for you, or would you prefer to do it yourself?

option.name: auto_generate
option.label: Generate Code Automatically
option.description: I will run the Gradle task to generate all necessary connector code and project structure.

option.name: manual_setup
option.label: Manual Setup
option.description: I will provide you with instructions to generate the code yourself.

sub_step_1_ask_generation_preference.presentation_format: Present options clearly: 1. Generate code automatically (I'll run the Gradle task) 2. Manual setup (You'll run the Gradle task yourself)

action.name: Navigate to sdk-gen Directory
action.note: Must change to sdk-gen directory before running Gradle task

action.name: Run Gradle Task
action.description: This Gradle task will: - Create the subproject: sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest - Generate all necessary Java source files - Set up the complete project structure - Create the resources directory at: sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest/src/main/resources

action.name: Verify Generation
action.description: Confirm that the following structure was created: - sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest/ - sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest/src/main/resources/

action.name: Inform User
action.message:
Connector code has been generated successfully.
Project location: sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest/ DSL files will be stored in: sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest/src/main/resources/
You can now modify the generated code if needed. Your DSL configuration files will be created in the resources directory.

action.name: Proceed to Configuration
action.description: Proceed to Phase 1 (API Discovery & Analysis) to gather API information and create configuration files.

if_gradle_fails.action: Report the error to the user
Ask if they want to try manual setup instead

action.name: Provide Instructions
action.instructions:
To generate the connector code manually, follow these steps:
1. Navigate to the sdk-gen directory: cd sdk-gen
2. Run the Gradle task to generate the connector code: ./gradlew generateJavaConnectorsForgeRest
3. This will create the following structure: - sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest/ - sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest/src/main/resources/
4. Once generated, you can modify the code as needed.
5. Your DSL configuration files will be stored in: sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest/src/main/resources/
After running these commands, let me know and I'll proceed with creating the REST API connector configuration files.

action.name: Wait for User Confirmation
action.description: Wait for the user to confirm they have completed the manual setup before proceeding to Phase 1.

action.name: Proceed to Configuration
action.description: Proceed to Phase 1 (API Discovery & Analysis) to gather API information and create configuration files.

dsl_file_location.title: DSL Configuration File Storage
dsl_file_location.description: Regardless of the deployment option chosen, all DSL configuration files (JSON connector descriptors) will be stored in the same location.

location.note: This is where all JSON configuration files created in subsequent phases will be written.

transition_to_next_phase.title: Transition to Phase 1
transition_to_next_phase.description: After completing deployment setup, transition to Phase 1 (API Discovery & Analysis).

prerequisites.prerequisite: For developer option with auto-generation: Code generated successfully
For developer option with manual setup: User confirmed completion

next_phase.description: Gather API information (base URL, authentication, endpoints, responses) and create JSON configuration files.

deployment_setup_phase.behavioral_rules: This phase MUST be completed before any API configuration work begins Do not skip deployment option selection Always create the project structure before proceeding <<<<<<< HEAD <<<<<<< HEAD <<<<<<< HEAD:.bob/rules-connectors-forge-rest/0_deployment_setup.xml For codeless option, inform user about pre-built Docker image (ghcr.io/marek-zuwala/connectors-forge:1.0.1) ======= For codeless option, inform user about pre-built Docker image (marek02/connectors-forge:latest) >>>>>>> 5b82670 (mode can do everything):.bob/rules-rest-api-schema-mapper/0_deployment_setup.xml ======= For codeless option, inform user about pre-built Docker image (ghcr.io/marek-zuwala/connectors-forge:1.0.0) >>>>>>> dacf80e (custom mode can able to deploy on openshift) ======= For codeless option, inform user about pre-built Docker image (ghcr.io/marek-zuwala/connectors-forge:1.0.1) >>>>>>> 9083115 (update image tag) For developer option, respect user's choice between auto and manual generation All DSL files go to: sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest/src/main/resources/ After setup is complete, proceed immediately to Phase 1

important_notes.note: <<<<<<< HEAD <<<<<<< HEAD <<<<<<< HEAD:.bob/rules-connectors-forge-rest/0_deployment_setup.xml The codeless option uses a pre-built Docker image (ghcr.io/marek-zuwala/connectors-forge:1.0.1) from GitHub Container Registry. ======= The codeless option uses a pre-built Docker image (marek02/connectors-forge:latest) from Docker Hub. >>>>>>> 5b82670 (mode can do everything):.bob/rules-rest-api-schema-mapper/0_deployment_setup.xml ======= The codeless option uses a pre-built Docker image (ghcr.io/marek-zuwala/connectors-forge:1.0.0) from GitHub Container Registry. >>>>>>> dacf80e (custom mode can able to deploy on openshift) ======= The codeless option uses a pre-built Docker image (ghcr.io/marek-zuwala/connectors-forge:1.0.1) from GitHub Container Registry. >>>>>>> 9083115 (update image tag) This image will be used during deployment instead of building a custom image.
The Gradle task 'generateJavaConnectorsForgeRest' must be run from the sdk-gen directory.
Both deployment options result in the same DSL file storage location.
This phase is independent of the API configuration process - it only sets up the deployment infrastructure.
